text: **Ashley:** Anna, where are you going in such a {hurry|急いで|/ˈhʌri/|She's in a hurry to catch the bus.}?
**Anna:** Oh hi Ashley, the nurse's {office|診療所|/ˈɔfɪs/|I'm going to the school office.}
**Anna:** Today I get the results from my {blood|血液|/blʌd/|A blood test checks your health.} test.
**Ashley:** I'm sure you're fine.
**Ashley:** Don't worry.
**Anna:** You know, I used to {dream|夢見る|/driːm/|I dream of becoming a doctor.} of being a nurse.
**Anna:** It is such an {honorable|名誉ある|/ˈɑnərəbəl/|A teacher has an honorable profession.} career.
**Anna:** Is there something you've dreamed of doing?
**Ashley:** Sure.
**Ashley:** I would love to be a {dog|犬|/dɔɡ/|My dog likes to play fetch.} trainer.
**Anna:** Actually, I've taught my dog Dublin lots of {tricks|芸/技|/trɪks/|Dogs can learn many tricks.}
**Anna:** Well, I'm off to the nurse.
**Anna:** Bye.
**Ashley:** Bye Anna.
**Anna:** Bye, Dublin.
**Nurse:** I'm looking at her test results, and they're not {good|良い/良好な|/ɡʊd/|The test results look good.}
**Nurse:** In fact, I'm afraid she's not going to {make|成功する/なる|/meɪk/|I hope she will make it.} it.
**Anna:** What?
**Anna:** I'm not going to make it.
**Anna:** I'm dying.
**Nurse:** Anna, come in.
**Nurse:** How are you?
**Anna:** I guess not so good I'm dying?
**Nurse:** What are you talking about?
**Anna:** I just heard you on the {phone|電話|/foʊn/|She's talking on the phone.}
**Anna:** Just tell me how much longer do I have?
**Nurse:** I wasn't talking about you.
**Nurse:** A student of mine didn't do well on her science {test|テスト/試験|/test/|I passed my math test.}
**Anna:** So she's not going to make it to {graduation|卒業|/ɡrædʒuˈeɪʃən/|Graduation is an important day.}
**Anna:** Woo
**Anna:** Thank goodness.
**Music:** For a minute?
**Anna:** My whole {life|人生} passed before my eyes.
**Anna:** There are so many things I haven't done yet.
**Anna:** Well {life|人生} is short.
**Anna:** It's important to do something you {love|愛する/大好きな} every day.
**Anna:** I couldn't agree more.
**Nurse:** Let's talk about your {blood|血液} test.
**Nurse:** Your {cholesterol|コレステロール} is really low.
**Anna:** Awesome!
**Jonathan:** Okay, Anna.
**Jonathan:** Say something and I'll check your {sound|音/音声} level
**Anna:** Testing.
**Anna:** One, two, three.
**Anna:** Testing.
**Anna:** Jonathan, I've been {wondering|不思議に思う/疑問に思う} something.
**Anna:** What's at the top of your {bucket|バケツ/目標} list
**Jonathan:** That's easy.
**Jonathan:** I'd love to win the World {Karaoke|カラオケ} Contest.
**Anna:** This is so {strange|奇妙な/変な}.
**Anna:** I just saw on Twitter
**Anna:** that they are having {auditions|オーディション} in DC this weekend.
**Jonathan:** Are you serious?
**Jonathan:** Can you read the story by yourself?
**Ashley:** Sure, absolutely.
**Jonathan:** Thanks.
**Anna:** You go
**Anna:** Penelope, I was {wondering|不思議に思う/疑問に思う} if you still dream of being a dancer.
**Penelope:** Only every day, well guess what?
**Anna:** A famous dancer is having a {flash|閃光/突然の} mob around the corner
**Anna:** {Hundreds|何百もの|/ˈhʌndrədz/|Hundreds of people came to the party.} of people are dancing together.
**Penelope:** No way.
**Anna:** If you {leave|去る|/liːv/|Please leave the room.} right now, you can join them.
**Anna:** Bye!
**Anna:** Pete, what's one of your {dreams|夢/希望|/driːmz/|I have big dreams for my future.}?
**Pete:** Well, last night I {dreamt|夢を見た|/drɛmt/|I dreamt about flying.} I was in a spaceship.
**Anna:** I was flying through {outer space|宇宙|/ˈaʊtər speɪs/|Astronauts explore outer space.} with these two beautiful Not that kind of dream.
**Anna:** I mean your hopes for the future.
**Anna:** Oh.
**Anna:** I plan on {selling|売る|/ˈsɛlɪŋ/|We are selling our old car.} my book to Hollywood and becoming rich and famous.
**Anna:** Oh, that's going to be {difficult|難しい|/ˈdɪfɪkəlt/|Math can be difficult sometimes.}.
**Pete:** It's my agent.
**Pete:** Hello?
**Pete:** Uh-huh.
**Ashley:** Sure
**Pete:** I can be in Los Angeles tomorrow.
**Anna:** Well, good job, Anna!
**Anna:** My work here is done.
**Anna:** Almost.
**Music:** You can search, but you won't find the answer without a dream You can reach for the star
**Music:** But you won't get there without a dream, without dre
**Anna:** Hannah, what's going on?
**Anna:** Where is everybody?
**Anna:** I don't know.
**Anna:** Uh maybe something is uh
**Anna:** Going around the office.
**Anna:** You mean {germs|細菌|/dʒɜːrmz/|Germs can make you sick.}?
**Anna:** Anna, you better use some of this hand cleaner.
**Anna:** You don't want to get sick too.
**Anna:** Right.
**Anna:** Okay, thanks.
**Anna:** Not {germs|細菌/病原体|/dʒɜːrmz/|Wash your hands to avoid germs.}
**Anna:** {Dreams|夢/目標|/driːmz/|I have big dreams for my future.}, Miss Weaver.
**Anna:** Dreams.
**Ashley:** What'd you say, Anna?
**Anna:** Nothing.
**Anna:** We hope you have {enjoyed|楽しんだ/満喫した|/ɪnˈdʒɔɪd/|I enjoyed the movie very much.} level two of Let's Learn English.
**Anna:** Everyone is following their {dreams|夢/目標|/driːmz/|Follow your dreams and never give up.}, big and small.
**Anna:** What are your {dreams|夢/目標|/driːmz/|Dreams can inspire us to achieve great things.}?
**Anna:** Our dream is for you to keep learning English.
**Anna:** Thanks for watching!
